programme.title: PBS News Compass Points
programme.desc: Join PBS News Hour's Nick Schifrin and a panel of experts each week analysing prominent international affairs topics and America's role in a shifting world, breaking down global conflicts, rising tensions, and their implications for U.S. interests.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News Hour
programme.desc: In-depth global news from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Doha, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: Finding Your Roots
programme.sub-title: To The Manor Born: Glenn Close, John Waters
programme.desc: Henry Louis Gates, Jr. uncovers the lineages of actor Glenn Close and director John Waters, digging into centuries past to encounter a succession of bold forebears who found fortune amidst drama and rebellion. From the gold rush fields of the Yukon to an enclave in Quaker Pennsylvania to British royalty, our guests meet a succession of relatives who prove to be as eccentric as they are.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Mark walks the pure white sands of WAs Esperance region, Rae cooks up a storm in Shepparton with a local legend, and Ernie shoots the breeze with a life long mate in Perth.

programme.title: Plat du Tour
programme.desc: The peloton is charging towards France's most beautiful medieval city, Carcassonne. The fortress here is the second most visited place in France (besides the Eiffel Tower, bien sur!) On Guillaume's menu is Le Carcassonnais Brioche, a dessert made with raisins and filled with deluxe creme patissiere. It's fitting tribute to the rich history of its namesake city where traditions stand as strong as its ancient stone walls.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: It's a spicy sausage spectacular, and the heat is on Adam's guests, chef Ruben Lopez Mesa and radio host Megan Burslem.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: Secrets of the Toxic Tomb
programme.desc: In 1922, the tomb of Egyptian boy king Tutankhamun was unearthed. Soon, tales spread of a deadly jinx. Now, Ella Al-Shamahi reveals the extraordinary scientific truth behind the 'Pharaoh's Curse'.

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Tonight, The Rush Hour Trilogy, Essendon Football Club, Katniss Evergreen from The Hunger Games and professional wrestler The Undertaker. Quite the mix. Contenders: Mark Summers, Neve D'Arcy, Damon Jakovics, Kelly Wickham.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Australia's Greek Cafes & Milk Bars
programme.desc: Long before global food chains arrived, Greek migrants shaped the way Australians ate, socialised and understood community. Shaken and Stirred tells the remarkable story of those who fled poverty and war to build a new life in Australia, facing prejudice and hardship along the way, yet leaving an extraordinary legacy. Drawing on rare archive footage and intimate family stories, this documentary asks what belonging really means and who gets to define it.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a brand new episode of 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown. Jon Richardson and Big Zuu take on Danny Dyer and Helen Bauer in the classic words and numbers quiz. Mat Ewins joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while maths whizz Rachel Riley looks after the numbers.

programme.title: Never Mind the Buzzcocks
programme.sub-title: Mel B, Alex James, Julian Clary, Sister Bliss
programme.desc: Never Mind the Buzzcocks takes on the 90's in this hour-long special. Spice Girl Mel B steps in for Daisy May Cooper as captain, joining host Greg Davies, Noel Fielding, and Jamali Maddix. Guests include Blur's Alex James, Sister Bliss from Faithless, and comedian Julian Clary, who claims he \.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Herrhausen: The Banker & The Bomb
programme.desc: A successful Moscow deal establishes Herrhausen as the most influential man in global finance, as well as the very top of Red Army Faction's hit-list. Aware of the target on his back, Herrhausen is seriously injured during a simulated terrorist attack. Yet Herrhausen persists in his mission to restructure Deutsche Bank, meeting the global financial crisis head on and taking risks that others would not dream of, yet each success only escalates his vulnerability.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: With Pietro Savastano having to leave the country and his son Gennaro's life hanging by a thread, Ciro de Marzio forges a new alliance with Don Salvatore Conte.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: Gennaro has established a new drug trafficking network in Central America. His reunion with his father Don Pietro marks a turning point in their relationship.

programme.title: Gomorrah
programme.desc: The alliance forged by Ciro and Conte is becoming fragile. When Conte takes vengeance on one of Ciro's men, the tide turns.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vet Jennie saves the life of a show chicken, Darren helps a pregnant cow avoid a caesarean section, and Maurice comes face to face with a strange visitor.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: This episode of Bamay showcases beautiful Arrernte and Warlpiri Country, with locations such as Mparntwe Alice Springs and the Ellery Creek Big Hole.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC 6 O'Clock News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: Matched
programme.sub-title: Raneet And Nirmansha+ Jenica And Deval
programme.desc: After Raneet proposes on a first date, he is suddenly thrust into the back seat as his parents take over deciding whether or not his fiance is a suitable match. And free-spirited bachelorette, Jenica, believes Indian men are not for her but will a trip to the motherland prove otherwise?

programme.title: Spectacular Railways Of The Swiss Alps
programme.desc: Carved by hand into the mountainside 130 years ago, the Brienzer Rothorn Railway climbs 7.6 kilometres from the emerald shores of Lake Brienz to a summit of 2244 metres. It is the only cogwheel railway in Switzerland to run a daily steam service, hauled by locomotives dating back to the 19th century. At the top, one of the most spectacular alpine panoramas in the world awaits.

programme.title: Scottish Vets Down Under
programme.desc: Dr Chris starts the day with an unusual commute before tackling a worrying lump in a much-loved family pet, with an unexpected twist during surgery. There's laughter in the field as he attempts to wrangle some uncooperative emus. At the equine hospital, Dr Mike tends to a very special pony whose work is changing lives. Later, a surprise visitor to the clinic leaves Dr Chris genuinely speechless.

programme.title: Scottish Vets Down Under
programme.desc: At the animal hospital, Dr Chris tends to a pair of fascinating reptiles and checks in on some recovering wildlife patients. At the equine hospital, a mare is rushed in with a life-threatening condition, forcing Dr Mike to race back for a gruelling emergency operation. It's one of the most demanding procedures an equine vet can face, and despite everyone's best efforts, the day takes a devastating turn.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: What Does Australia Really Think About
programme.sub-title: What Does Australia Really Think About Disability?
programme.desc: Kurt Fearnley has represented Australia at five Paralympic games, won the New York marathon five times and has even crawled the Kokoda Trail. Now, he's embarking on his biggest challenge yet: he wants to change the way Australia thinks about disability. He investigates what Australia really thinks about disability through emotional personal stories, confronting social experiments, covert filming and an in-depth nationwide survey.

programme.title: Trail Towns
programme.desc: Do you want glistening beaches dotted with quintessential Aussie surf towns that are home to some of the newest trail networks in Australia? Yes please. Eurobodalla captivates Vandy and Deetz like a couple of meerkats at feeding time.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Tonight, Adam and his guests, social media stars Katie Lolas and Jasmin Weston are lifting the bread on some incredibly delicious sandwich secrets.

programme.title: Tutankhamun: Secrets of the Toxic Tomb
programme.desc: Ella Al-Shamahi dives deeper into the mystery of the 'Pharaoh's Curse'. Was King Tut's tomb lethally toxic? Or was the real curse a case of fatal obsession?

programme.title: Jeopardy!
programme.desc: Ken Jennings hosts Jeopardy! Where the answers are revealed, and the contestants must guess the appropriate questions.

programme.title: Letters and Numbers
programme.desc: Two contestants pit their linguistic and numerical skills against each other and the clock in this entertaining quiz show. Join host Richard Morecroft along with maths whiz Lily Serna and wordsmith David Astle.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: Tonight, a line-up that spans galaxies, genres and ancient curses: the Planets of the Solar System, singer-songwriter Chappell Roan, One Piece manga, and The Mummy trilogy. Contenders: Kathryn Trower, James Spedding, Vanessa Stephens, Scott Charman.

programme.title: SBS World News
programme.desc: The latest reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Zweisimmen To Lausanne/ Nantes To Lorient
programme.desc: Aboard the Golden Pass Express, Michael Portillo wends his way in style down steep mountainsides from Interlaken to the Swiss Riviera. In Lausanne, he contends with a knife-wielding robotic arm at a renowned centre of science and technology. Michael is impressed by the robot's skills in the kitchen, before witnessing a groundbreaking prosthetic that allows amputees to feel heat and cold.

programme.title: Insight
programme.desc: Triplets are rare, with fewer than 60 sets of triplets born in Australia each year. Insight explores what life's like for those individuals and those around them, asking how easy is it to be oneself and what happens to the broader family when triplets grow up in the mix?

programme.title: Dateline
programme.desc: Struggling to adapt to life in the South, many North Korean defectors turn to matchmaking services seeking security through marriage. But these agencies have faced criticism for promoting patriarchy.

programme.title: SBS World News Late
programme.desc: Reports and analysis of major national and international news of the day from Australia's only world news service.

programme.title: Living Black
programme.desc: Living Black returns in 2026 with another series of in depth conversations and investigations. Join Walkley Award winner Karla Grant as she explores the issues facing Indigenous Australians.

programme.title: Exit
programme.desc: After a year off the grid, William makes a dramatic return, diving headfirst back into familiar excess. Back in Norway following a heady spell in France, the group quickly falls into old routines. Hermine, however, is playing a longer game, forging new alliances - and Adam may be heading for troubled times.

programme.title: Clean Sweep
programme.desc: Shelly Mohan, a housewife and mother of three, makes an irreversible decision, and commits an unspeakable act, when a man from her distant past surfaces, she commits murder.

programme.title: Clean Sweep
programme.desc: After realising that she might have left crucial evidence in Charlie's hotel room, Shelly devises a plan to destroy it discreetly at the police station.

programme.title: Clean Sweep
programme.desc: We learn of Shelly's dark history, as a teenager who was part of a drug ring in London 20 years earlier with Charlie Lynch, the man she shot. When Lynch's true identity is uncovered, a UK officer, DSI Crichett, reopens an old murder case Shelly and Charlie were linked to.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mastermind Australia
programme.desc: The legendary quiz that sets the toughest questions on television. Join quizmaster Marc Fennell as each night he puts four contenders to the test.

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vet Jennie must save a litter of puppies, Nuala carries out an important job on her family farm, and an emergency case leaves Darren delivering two sets of twins.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: In part 3 of Tasmania's Northwest, we explore coastal tracks and windswept beaches met with cultural memory. From The Nut, ancient volcanic plug to Perkins Bay, this episode reflects on the interconnection between land, sea, and identity.

programme.title: NHK World English News Morning
programme.desc: English news from Japan's Broadcasting Corporation, NHK, delivering the latest in business, technology, health, environmental and world news.

programme.title: ANC Philippines The World Tonight
programme.desc: Delivers the top stories of the day, covering political, economic, and social sectors on both local and global fronts.

programme.title: DW English News
programme.desc: News and analysis of the top international and European news and current affairs from Berlin, in English.

programme.title: Al Jazeera News
programme.desc: News via satellite from Al Jazeera Satellite Network, Qatar, in English.

programme.title: BBC 6 O'Clock News
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 English News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: DD India News Hour
programme.desc: The most recent political, sports, entertainment and socio-economic updates across India and the world from New Delhi, in English.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw Bitesize
programme.desc: Join host Adam Liaw and all your favourite chefs, cooks and celebrities for the best bits from The Cook Up kitchen.

programme.title: Matched
programme.sub-title: Radhika And Gurvinder+ Harmeek And Sukpreet
programme.desc: Hindu bride-to-be Radhika and Sikh groom-to-be Gurvinder must compromise to honour two cultures, but it's not easy mixing ancient traditions, especially when it comes to marriage. Harmeek and Sukpreet are having a fully traditional arranged marriage, but will they find love after the wedding?

programme.title: Spectacular Railways Of The Swiss Alps
programme.desc: South of Lucerne rises one of Switzerland's most dramatic mountain massifs, and for over a century the world's steepest cogwheel railway has been the most exhilarating way to reach its summit. Mount Pilatus is a journey that rewards the brave, combining breathtaking alpine scenery with a ride that delivers a genuine thrill from the very first metre. This is landscape cinema at its most spectacular.

programme.title: Scottish Vets Down Under
programme.desc: It's a Saturday in Bendigo, but there's no rest for the teams. At the animal hospital, Dr Chris treats a beloved working dog whose farm injury has turned serious, and a very special wildlife patient arrives carefully hidden from the world. At the equine hospital, Dr Mike and Dr Sarah face a tense race to save a four-day-old colt, but the battle is far from over once the operation is complete.

programme.title: Scottish Vets Down Under
programme.desc: It's foaling season, but not every birth goes to plan. At the equine hospital, Dr Mike and Dr Sarah race to save a newborn colt whose difficult arrival has left him unable to stand or feed. At the animal hospital, Dr Clare tackles a surprisingly common springtime hazard causing real harm to local pets. Later, Dr Chris sees three of his favourite patients, while Dr Mike enjoys a well-earned day off.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news stories from the BBC News team from London, UK, in English.

programme.title: France 24 International News
programme.desc: International news and current affairs television from Paris, offering a French perspective on world events, in English.

programme.title: PBS NewsHour
programme.desc: Reviews and analysis of the day's news presented by Amna Nawaz and Geoff Bennett for the Public Broadcasting Service (PBS) in the United States.

programme.title: Dateline
programme.desc: Struggling to adapt to life in the South, many North Korean defectors turn to matchmaking services seeking security through marriage. But these agencies have faced criticism for promoting patriarchy.
